title: Appropriate char types should be preferred for character and integer values
description: Appropriate char types should be used for character and integer valuesAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misracertconfusingWhy is this an issue?More InfoThere are three distinctchartypes, (plain)char,signed charandunsigned char.signed charandunsigned charshould only be used for numeric data, and plaincharshould only be used for character data. Since it is implementation-defined, the signedness of the plainchartype should not be assumed.Noncompliant code examplesigned char a = 'a'; // Noncompliant, explicitly signed unsigned char b = '\r'; // Noncompliant, explicitly unsigned char c = 10; // Noncompliant unsigned char d = c; // Noncompliant, d is explicitly signed while c is not char e = a; // Noncompliant, a is explicitly signed while e is notCompliant s
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant: signed char a = 'a'; // Noncompliant, explicitly signed
unsigned char b = '\r'; // Noncompliant, explicitly unsigned
char c = 10; // Noncompliant, plain char for numeric data
unsigned char d = c; // Noncompliant, mixing signed/unsigned
compliant: char a = 'a'; // Compliant, plain char for character data
char b = '\r'; // Compliant, plain char for character data
signed char c = 10; // Compliant, signed char for numeric data
unsigned char d = 255; // Compliant, unsigned char for numeric data

signed_char_with_character.message: signed char should not be used with character literals - use plain char instead

unsigned_char_with_character.message: unsigned char should not be used with character literals - use plain char instead

plain_char_with_numeric.message: plain char should not be used with numeric literals - use signed/unsigned char instead

signed_char_string.message: signed char should not be used for strings - use plain char instead

unsigned_char_string.message: unsigned char should not be used for strings - use plain char instead
